_meta.purpose: Falsification ledger for the crossval research lane (issue #273 revival). One row per hypothesis that was killed or kept during the 2026-07-04..07-07 patch cross-validation campaigns. Numbers were re-checked against the cited artifacts before writing (2026-07-10).

fields.campaign: which cross-validation campaign the hypothesis belongs to
fields.hypothesis: the claim under test, stated so it can be falsified
fields.verdict: KILL = falsified; KEEP = survived; KEEP-freq = survived for the dip FREQUENCY only
fields.numbers: the deciding numbers, verbatim from the artifacts
fields.evidence: lane-relative artifact paths (small copies live in evidence/; out_*/ is gitignored)
fields.caveats: honest scope limits, including anything still OPEN

_meta.conventions: dip frequency is mesh-limited and an unstable argmin -> never a gate (tests/test_issue80_patch_s11_regression.py); dip depth is not comparable across solvers while loss models differ

verdicts.hypothesis: A serial coarse-mesh Meep ring-down (res=2 cells/mm, ~3.0M cells) can act as the third solver for the canonical 2.4 GHz FR4 patch resonance
verdicts.numbers: r2 (1200 Meep-units ring-down) and r2b (600 units): no qualifying Harminv resonance (harminv_modes empty, mode-shape check 0 sign changes along x vs TM010 expecting 1); r3 (finer attempt): killed by timeout (EXIT 124, no JSON artifact)
verdicts.caveats: This Meep build is serial (non-MPI), which caps affordable resolution; 2 cells/mm under-resolves the h=1.5 mm substrate. Kills THIS configuration as a third solver, not Meep in general. r3 verdict is log-only (rfx-ref scripts/crossval/out/r3.log tail).

verdicts.hypothesis: The r4 Meep configuration's dominant ring-down mode lands in the 2.40-2.56 GHz cross-solver target window
verdicts.numbers: dominant mode 3.0044 GHz, Q=7.3: +20.8% vs openEMS 2.487 GHz, +17.7% vs rfx 2.553 GHz, +24.0% vs Balanis 2.424 GHz -> OUT OF WINDOW; mode-shape check fails TM010 (0 sign changes along x, y_min/max=-1.07)
verdicts.caveats: Q=7.3 is far below a patch-cavity Q and the mode shape is not TM010 -- the selected mode is likely a box/feed artifact, so the +20% offset says nothing about rfx/openEMS.

verdicts.hypothesis: A one-port MSL line run into the far CPML is a valid matched-thru fixture for the X-band frame (the PR #272 wiring)
verdicts.numbers: dip -0.567 dB, max|S11|=1.0053 over 1.5-15 GHz -- an essentially total reflection record with a marginal passivity overshoot, not a matched thru
verdicts.caveats: PR #272 withdrawn. The pec-pad thru variant survives only as evidence/rfx_patch_inset_thru_pecpad.json (artifact-only; its reproducing thru branch was deleted in this correction -- no script regenerates it). The canonical matched-thru is scripts/diagnostics/msl_thru_mesh_convergence.py (two add_msl_port ends, z_lo PEC ground boundary; pattern of tests/test_msl_port_integration.py).

verdicts.hypothesis: rfx's 1-cell-thick metal (vs the openEMS zero-thickness sheet) explains the rfx-vs-openEMS dip disagreement
verdicts.numbers: openEMS sheet: 9.2625 GHz / -9.46 dB; openEMS 197um-thick slab: 9.2625 GHz / -10.33 dB -- dip frequency unmoved, depth shift ~0.9 dB only
verdicts.caveats: Tested on the openEMS side (thickness added there); assumes the thickness effect is solver-portable.

verdicts.hypothesis: The rfx -30.25 dB @ 10.359 GHz deep match (dx=197um, interior-ground frame) is a physical 50-ohm match point
verdicts.numbers: dx=197um: -30.25 dB @ 10.359 GHz -> dx=98.5um: -12.02 dB @ 10.106 GHz -> canonical frame (z_lo PEC ground): -7.04 dB @ 9.250 GHz. The deep null was a coarse-mesh + miswired-frame coincidence
verdicts.caveats: This retracts the 10.1 GHz claims that palace_patch/README.md originally set out to arbitrate. Dip depth is an unstable argmin observable; never gate on it.

verdicts.hypothesis: In the shared shielded (all-PEC) box the three solvers agree on the fundamental resonance
verdicts.numbers: rfx Harminv 9.131 GHz / openEMS ring-down 9.194 GHz / Palace eigenmode 9.199 GHz -- 0.7% spread; second mode 10.764 / 10.799 / 10.797-10.806 GHz
verdicts.caveats: The openEMS 9.194 GHz is the Harminv mode of the port-voltage ring-down tail (printed at run time; blessed in DESIGN_DOC_patch_student.md). It is NOT re-derivable from the committed shielded JSON, whose stored ratio-curve dip (~9.77 GHz) is a different observable -- the number's provenance is the run log. The shielded openEMS JSON's max|S11|=7.61 is NOT a passivity failure -- S11 is undefined for the closed-box ring-down (see openems_patch_inset_xband.py --shielded). The rfx artifact also carries below-window Harminv lines at 6.627/6.791 GHz (outside the requested 7-12 GHz band; Harminv conventionally returns out-of-band poles, and the Palace eigensolve Target=8 GHz could neither corroborate nor refute them -- uncharacterized, not part of the agreement claim).

verdicts.hypothesis: With open boundaries the three solvers agree on the radiating S11 dip
verdicts.numbers: dip frequency: rfx 9.250 GHz (dx=197um canonical frame, -7.04 dB) / openEMS 9.2625 GHz (-9.46 dB) / Palace driven 9.05 GHz (-12.99 dB, caveated: tight first-order absorbing box pulls the driven dip low; its shielded eigenmode agrees at 9.199 GHz). Depth spread -7.0 / -9.5 / -13.0 dB stays OPEN while loss models differ
verdicts.caveats: OPEN: the canonical-frame dx=98.5um rfx run shows max|S11|=1.3579 (evidence/rfx_cf_s11_dx98um.json) -- an unresolved fine-mesh passivity violation, carried here, not hidden. Depth is not comparable across solvers until the loss models are unified (board tan-delta ~0.0027 per DESIGN_DOC_patch_student.md; the FDTD legs and the corrected Palace config are lossless by convention). Dip frequency itself is mesh-limited and never a gate.
